mnemonics: Speedup and efficiency in MIMD systems: S.I.M.P.S.
Taking timings of MIMD programs: T.T.O.M.P.
keyPoints: S.I.M.P.S. = Number of processors x Processor speed / Maximum number of processors x Maximum processor speed
A.L.A.M.O.S. = 1 - B / N, where B is the fraction of the program that is independent and N is the number of processors
S.S.S.S. = Number of processors x Processor speed / Maximum number of processors x Maximum processor speed
T.T.O.M.P. = To measure the time taken by a program on multiple processors, start with the original program and then simulate the execution of the program on multiple processors
G.P.U.P.E. = Global memory access rate, memory bandwidth, and processor performance
